mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over consecutive 3-year rolling windows or positive returns exceed negative returns in more than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, which boosts near-term corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series signals or positive return frequency drops below 50 percent when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the index of industrial production reflects accelerating manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals immediate earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns during recovery phases or the 5-day return correlation with INDPRO changes falls below 0.05 in rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth rates reflect expanding economic activity and corporate revenue bases, which systematically support equity valuations and yield positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over consecutive 3-year periods or positive return frequency falls below 45 percent across all GDP growth regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally on high inflation prints or the negative return frequency drops below 40 percent when the threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal underperforms a single-series baseline or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of co-occurring instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals a contraction in residential construction and related supply chains, which reduces near-term industrial demand and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases or the 5-day return correlation with HOUST changes remains statistically indistinguishable from zero.
